text.title: Experimentation at Scale Core Rules
text.summary: Hypothesis-driven A/B experimentation program (>=100 experiments/year) with pre-registered metrics, guardrails, statistical rigor, and an agent-assisted triage -> readout -> archive loop.

rule.statement: Every experiment MUST have a written hypothesis with primary metric, secondary metrics, guardrails, MDE, and stop conditions registered BEFORE traffic is sent. No post-hoc primary swaps.

rationale.source: Kohavi et al — Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments; GrowthBook docs

rule.rationale: Post-hoc primary swap is the #1 source of false positives in mature programs.

rule.statement: Each experiment ships with >=3 guardrail metrics (revenue/user, error rate, latency, retention). Any guardrail crossing its floor auto-pauses the experiment regardless of primary lift.
rule.rationale: Guardrails catch unintended damage that a single primary metric can mask.

rule.statement: Sample-size and runtime calc MUST be run before launch; experiments shipped with insufficient power are forbidden. Document the MDE assumption in the hypothesis doc.
rule.rationale: Under-powered experiments produce noise that the org learns to ignore, killing the program.

rule.statement: Sample Ratio Mismatch (SRM) check at p<0.001 MUST run before any readout. SRM failure invalidates the readout — debug bucketing, do not interpret results.
rule.rationale: SRM points to bucketing / instrumentation bugs that make ALL downstream stats meaningless.

rule.statement: Every readout ends with a binary action: ship / kill / iterate. Inconclusive results auto-route to iterate or kill; never 'hold' indefinitely.
rule.rationale: Holding inconclusive experiments creates a queue of zombie variants and drains experiment slots.

rule.statement: An agent ranks experiment proposals by expected-value-per-week before slot allocation; duplicates against past experiments are auto-rejected unless market context changed.
rule.rationale: Without triage, slot allocation becomes politics and throughput collapses.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — low-traffic product where MDE detection takes >4 weeks; irreversible high-risk change; legally restricted variant; team without analytics platform), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
